uses: Cracca astragalina, also known as Huang qi, is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb used to enhance the immune system and improve overall health. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and asthma. The herb is also used to improve digestion and reduce fatigue. In addition, Cracca astragalina is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote wound healing. It is often used in combination with other herbs to create a tonic that supports the body's natural defenses.

constituents: Cracca astragalina, also known as Huang Qi, is a traditional Chinese medicinal plant used for various health benefits. The active constituents of Cracca astragalina include astragalosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids. preparations: Cracca astragalina, also known as Huang Qi or Astragalus, is a traditional herb used in Chinese medicine for its immune-boosting properties. Herbal preparations of Cracca astragalina typically involve drying and processing the roots of the plant, which are then combined with other herbs to create a decoction or tea. intro: Cracca astragalina, also known as Huang Qi, is a flowering plant belonging to the legume family Fabaceae, native to China and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to promote health and wellness, with reported health benefits including immune system support, anti-inflammatory effects, and improved digestion. The bioactive constituents of Cracca astragalina include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cracca astragalina include dried root extracts, capsules, and teas, often used to treat conditions such as gastrointestinal disorders and autoimmune diseases.
